ORDER DENYING PLAINTIFF SAN SAN AUNG'S MOTIONS TO STRIKE DEFENDANTS THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA AND CONRAD PITHEAU MEMORANDUM OF COSTS

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF SAN AUNG'S MOTION TO STRIKE MEMORANDUM OF COSTS.PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO STRIKE MEMORANDUM OF COSTS IS DENIED. CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 3.1700(A)(1) PROVIDES: A PREVAILING PARTY WHO CLAIMS COSTS MUST SERVE AND FILE A MEMORANDUM OF COSTS WITHIN 15 DAYS AFTER THE DATE OF SERVICE OF THE NOTICE OF ENTRY OF JUDGMENT OR DISMISSAL BY THE CLERK UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 664.5 OR THE DATE OF SERVICE OF WRITTEN NOTICE OF ENTRY OF JUDGMENT OR DISMISSAL, OR WITHIN 180 DAYS AFTER ENTRY OF JUDGMENT, WHICHEVER IS FIRST.DEFENDANT THE REGENTS FILED AND SERVED NOTICE OF ENTRY OF JUDGMENT ON NOVEMBER 15, 2022. (SEE BARRICK DECL, EX 1.) DEFENDANTS FILED THEIR MEMORANDUMS OF COSTS AND DISBURSEMENTS ON NOVEMBER 29, 2022, AND NOVEMBER 30, 2022, WHICH WAS WITHIN THE 15 DAYS, AFTER THE DATE OF SERVICE OF THE NOTICE OF ENTRY OF JUDGMENT. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF SAN AUNG'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. OFF CALENDAR. NO PROOF OF SERVICE. IN ADDITION, THE MOTION WAS NOT FILED AND SERVED IN ACCORDANCE WITH CCP 1005. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Mar-20-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Plaintiff SAN SAN AUNG (Tel: 415-812-3139) in pro per;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT CONRAD PITHEAU's Motion For Attorneys Fees.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Conrad Piteau's "motion for attorneys' fees" is denied.

San San Aung is a food service worker who filed a pro per FEHA complaint naming her employer (UC Regents) and boss (Piteau). Later represented by a lawyer, Aung lost on summary judgment. With the lawyer's withdrawal, she is pro per again. In court, Aung has appeared unsophisticated and confused.

Government Code 12965(c)(6) gives a court discretion to award attorney fees to a prevailing FEHA defendant. I decline to exercise that discretion here for three reasons.

First, Piteau insists he is "entitled" to $48,982.50 in attorney fees from Aung "based on the frivolous nature" of her claims. (Memo. 5:20-21; Govt. Code 12965(c)(6).) The crux of his argument is that individuals are not liable under FEHA. However, Piteau concedes that FEHA harassment claims "may be asserted against an individual," and that Aung filed one. (Id. at 8:25-28.)

Second, Piteau invites the court to simply reduce his fee demand "by 25%" to "account for" the harassment claim. (Id.) But it was for Piteau's attorneys to reasonably allocate fees among the four claims, not for the court to make a rough cut on the fly.

Third, Aung's ability to pay $48,982.50 is also a consideration. (See Rosenman v. Miller, Fink, Jacobs, Glaser, Weil & Shapiro (2001) 91 Cal.App.4th 859, 868 n.42.) Aung may lack the sophistiction to make an impecunity showing, but the fact that she is a food service worker speaks for itself. Moreover, Piteau's memorandum does not say he has had to pay any attorney fees.

The prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Motion To Strike Memorandum Of Costs
Off Calendar Jan-05-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (by Zoom):
Oshea Orchid, Esq. specially appearing 415-410-9931 for Plaintiff.
Cara Barrick, Esq. 415-442-4810 OGLETREE, DEAKINS, NASH, SMOAK & STEWART, P.C.() for Defendant.
Steven Shaw, Esq. (916) 258-8800 SLOAN SAKAI YEUNG & WONG LLPfor Defendant CONRAD PITHEAU

RULING - A R G U E D. Ms. Orchid requests a continuance since the attorney is out sick with Covid-19. the Court informs parties there are only three alternatives. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF SAN AUNG's Motion To Strike Memorandum Of Costs.

Plaintiff's motion to strike memorandum of costs is denied.

California Rule of Court 3.1700(a)(1) provides: A prevailing party who claims costs must serve and file a memorandum of costs within 15 days after the date of service of the notice of entry of judgment or dismissal by the clerk under Code of Civil Procedure section 664.5 or the date of service of written notice of entry of judgment or dismissal, or within 180 days after entry of judgment, whichever is first.

Defendant The Regents filed and served notice of entry of judgment on November 15, 2022. (See Barrick Decl, Ex 1.) Defendants filed their memorandums of costs and disbursements on November 29, 2022, and November 30, 2022, which was within the 15 days, after the date of service of the notice of entry of judgment.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/rbu)
